steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the choice that best describes how DLS cures 3D objects — the mechanism, not a definition of the technology.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — DLS cures 3D objects by passing through a "dead zone," adding layers to the object, then curing the object in an oven.: Omits the driving mechanism (UV light projecting into resin), leaving the process incomplete. ✗
  • Choice B — In DLS, UV light is projected into layers of liquid resin until the resin solidifies and passes through a "dead zone," wherein the curing is completed.: Confuses the dead zone's role and incorrectly states that curing finishes there rather than in the oven. ✗
  • Choice C — In DLS, UV light images are projected into a liquid resin pool to cure a 3D object layer by layer; once solidified, the object is baked in an oven.: Directly and accurately describes the mechanism step by step. ✓
  • Choice D — DLS is a form of additive manufacturing that rapidly cures liquid resin into a 3D object in which UV light solidifies layer by layer before being baked in an oven, creating a high-quality 3D object.: Opens with a definition rather than the process and uses awkward construction that dilutes the explanation. ✗
That is the correct answer.
